title: Switch HTTPConnection from http.client to urllib3.connection
body:
This is required for compatibillity with urllib3 2.0+

Fixes: #281,#287

comments.body:
Well, sadly I'm now stuck with:
```
>           raise APIError(uri.geturl(), explanation=f"{method.upper()} operation failed") from e
E           podman.errors.exceptions.APIError: http://%2Frun%2Fapi.sock/v4.5.0/libpod/networks/podman (DELETE operation failed)

```
and I have frankly no idea how to solve that :-(

> Well, sadly I'm now stuck with:
> 
> ```
> >           raise APIError(uri.geturl(), explanation=f"{method.upper()} operation failed") from e
> E           podman.errors.exceptions.APIError: http://%2Frun%2Fapi.sock/v4.5.0/libpod/networks/podman (DELETE operation failed)
> ```
> 
> and I have frankly no idea how to solve that :-(

https://github.com/jamielennox/requests-mock/issues/228 would imply the latest things are still in flux.

> [jamielennox/requests-mock#228](https://github.com/jamielennox/requests-mock/issues/228) would imply the latest things are still in flux.

The latest release of requests-mock solved this. I also unpinned the requirement on urllib3, as that is not available for python 3.6.

changes LGTM
@jwhonce PTAL

I think merging this will (eventually) automatically close https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/287 ?  In either case, you could #287 to the `fixes` list in the title to guarantee it :grin: 

Thanks for taking on this work.

> @dcermak Is urllib3 not available for python 3.6 or just not this latest version?

The 2.x version is not available for python 3.6, but the previous versions are.

> I think merging this will (eventually) automatically close https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/287 ? In either case, you could https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/287 to the fixes list in the title to guarantee it

Done
FYI: Renovate just pushed an update to [its PR w/ slight version bumps](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/287/files).

> > > [jamielennox/requests-mock#228](https://github.com/jamielennox/requests-mock/issues/228) would imply the latest things are still in flux.
> > 
> > 
> > The latest release of requests-mock solved this. I also unpinned the requirement on urllib3, as that is not available for python 3.6.
> 
> @dcermak Is urllib3 not available for python 3.6 or just not this latest version?
> 
> @TomSweeneyRedHat @umohnani8 We were supporting py36 for RHEL8 is that platform still desired?
> 
> /cc @lsm5

@TomSweeneyRedHat is Rhel 8 still desired?

> > > > [jamielennox/requests-mock#228](https://github.com/jamielennox/requests-mock/issues/228) would imply the latest things are still in flux.
> > > 
> > > 
> > > The latest release of requests-mock solved this. I also unpinned the requirement on urllib3, as that is not available for python 3.6.
> > 
> > 
> > @dcermak Is urllib3 not available for python 3.6 or just not this latest version?
> > @TomSweeneyRedHat @umohnani8 We were supporting py36 for RHEL8 is that platform still desired?
> > /cc @lsm5
> 
> @TomSweeneyRedHat is Rhel 8 still desired?

yes, we're still shipping on rhel8

> yes, we're still shipping on rhel8

But it's not the "latest" upstream code/version though right?  So perhaps the updated deps here don't impact "old" RHEL/CentOS releases.  Just "new" ones.

(Note: These are only guesses, I have no actual knowledge of what I speak :grin:)
@lsm5 @umohnani8 Is this ready to merge?
@jnovy @TomSweeneyRedHat could you PTAL RE: rhel builds ? I'm happy as long as centos stream builds fine on packit, but will defer to Jindrich and Tom for the actual rhel case.

@jwhonce @umohnani8 "We were supporting py36 for RHEL8 is that platform still desired?" I have not heard otherwise.

@lsm5 this LGTM, but I'm way at the end of my knowledge base with this.  @jnovy any concerns?
Python3.6 seems to be the main Python workhorse in RHEL8. Is there a way ohw to make this PR compatible with python3.6 too?

> Python3.6 seems to be the main Python workhorse in RHEL8. Is there a way ohw to make this PR compatible with python3.6 too?

But it is, the package still happily builds on CentOS stream 8.
LGTM in that case.

/approve
/lgtm

Fantastic, thanks everybody for helping figure this update out!
